summary: Completed comprehensive testing of compulsory items feature, delete job functionality, and manager role access. Backend: 16/16 tests passed (100%). Frontend: Manager page, Admin page, and job CRUD operations working correctly.

issues: Uploaded asset templates (Tractor, HGV, etc.) don't have compulsory items - this is expected as the original Excel file didn't have a 'Compulsory' column. Only the default daily_check and grader_startup templates have compulsory items.

passed_tests: Backend: Employee login with admin credentials (4444)
Backend: GET /api/checklist-templates/daily_check returns items with compulsory field (4 compulsory items)
Backend: GET /api/checklist-templates/grader_startup returns items with compulsory field (4 compulsory items)
Backend: POST /api/checklists rejects checklist when compulsory items are unsatisfactory
Backend: POST /api/checklists accepts checklist when compulsory items are satisfactory
Backend: POST /api/checklists accepts checklist when non-compulsory items are unsatisfactory
Backend: Rejection message includes multiple failed compulsory items
Backend: Manager role returned in login response
Backend: Validate endpoint returns manager_control field
Backend: GET /api/assets/makes returns list of makes
action_items: IMPORTANT: Uploaded asset templates (Tractor, HGV, Monthly Inspections, etc.) don't have compulsory items because the original AssetList.xlsx file doesn't have a 'Compulsory' column. To enable compulsory items for these templates, user needs to re-upload the Excel file with a 'Compulsory' column (values: yes/y/true/1/x) in the checklist sheets.
critical_code_review_comments: Backend compulsory validation is correctly implemented at line 572-586 in server.py
Frontend compulsory UI indicators (red asterisk, COMPULSORY label, red border) are correctly implemented at lines 1825-1870 in App.js
Frontend submit button disabling logic is correctly implemented at lines 1340-1361 in App.js
Default templates (daily_check, grader_startup) have compulsory items defined at lines 1209-1239 in server.py
seed_data_creation: Created and deleted test jobs during testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Compulsory feature is fully implemented in backend and frontend code. Backend correctly rejects checklists with failed compulsory items. Frontend shows visual indicators (red asterisk, COMPULSORY label, red border) and disables submit button. However, the uploaded asset templates from AssetList.xlsx don't have compulsory flags because the Excel file doesn't have a 'Compulsory' column. Only the default daily_check and grader_startup templates have compulsory items. To test compulsory UI in frontend, either: 1) Create an asset with check_type='daily_check', or 2) Re-upload AssetList.xlsx with a 'Compulsory' column in the checklist sheets.
